text: Yesterday I talked about the Minecraft

addition to Minecraft they rolled out

to others. You know, a silence brand
moment and especially even more so
because the education edition is
specifically targeted at children as
Minecraft and Microsoft advocate for

used during certain classes at school.
In particular, I went to the website for

information about the team behind it,

really doing a great job of
inadvertently promoting homeschooling.
As many people would say that those
educators, they uh don't exactly seem
like very upstanding individuals. If you
want more context into what I'm talking
about, feel free to check out
yesterday's video. It's only like 2
minutes long and you'll very quickly see
what I'm referring to. At the end of
that video though, I showed a series of
tweets from Face Connect VTuber Jelly
Hoshi who was criticizing Minecraft for
that DLC. And while she got over 20,000
likes and a lot of people supporting her
take, there was also a lot of people
very upset with her in the replies,
finding her take to be offensive. And
this morning we have a statement, an
official statement from Faze Connect
official where they are condemning that
VTuber of theirs, Hosi, and the
statements that she made. They say,
"Thank you for supporting FaZe Connect.
Recent comments made on Twitter by one
of our talents including discussions on
sensitive issues." Following an internal
review, these comments were taken down.
We sincerely apologize for any issue
caused by this incident. FaZe Connect's
focus has been and always will be
providing entertainment through
different personalities and a wide range
of unique content. We do not tolerate
racism, hatred, or bigotry in our
communities. Thank you for understanding
and your continued support of Faze
Connect and our talents. FaZe Connect
team. FaZe also seems to have either
logged into Hosumi's account and deleted
her take directly or forced her to
delete it herself, although since she
was apparently live when it got deleted,
many are speculating that the former is
what happened. Nevertheless, now
management is getting criticized from
both the people who found Hosimi's take
to be offensive and those who didn't
find it offensive. As those who were
offended view their statement as face
admitting it was offensive and saying
that they don't support that behavior
but still employing that talent, well,
they view that as it coming off like
hollow words. And the people who don't
find it offensive view it as management
now stabbing Hosimi in the back to
appease a Twitter mob. Many people are
also saying that all management really
had to do was wait a short amount of
time for people to simply move on. But
now management has really got themselves
stuck between a rock and a hard place,
